@updates.checkToast.errorPrefix.description: Line in the menu-triggered update-check toast shown when the check failed, prefixing the raw error detail.

placeholders.message: the underlying error message text from the failed update check

@updates.checkToast.sendErrorReport.description: Button in the update-check toast that opens the error-report flow after a failed update check. Tight mini button.

updates.toast.available: New version available. Restart to update.

@updates.toast.available.description: Body of the persistent toast prompting the user to restart and apply a downloaded update.

@updates.toast.later.description: Button that dismisses the update-available toast without restarting now. Tight mini button.

@updates.toast.restart.description: Button that restarts the app immediately to apply the downloaded update. Tight mini button.

updates.status.noUpdates: No updates found. Current version: v{version}

@updates.status.noUpdates.description: Status line after a successful update check that found nothing newer, reporting the current version. The v prefix before the version number is literal.

placeholders.version: the current app version number (for example 0.23.1)

@updates.status.checking.description: Status line shown while an update check is in progress. Ends with a single ellipsis character.

@updates.status.downloading.description: Status line shown while a found update is downloading, naming the new and current versions. The v prefixes are literal; ends with a single ellipsis character.

placeholders.next: the new version being downloaded (for example 0.24.0)

@updates.status.installing.description: Status line shown while a downloaded update is installing, naming the new and current versions. The v prefixes are literal; ends with a single ellipsis character.

placeholders.next: the new version being installed (for example 0.24.0)

updates.status.ready: Update v{next} ready. Restart to apply.

@updates.status.ready.description: Status line shown when an update is downloaded and ready, prompting a restart. The v prefix is literal.
